global_rules: Complete Gate 173 before any src/ runtime edits in Gates 174-178.
Read every materially changed file end to end before claiming semantic alignment.
Do not let the new lane become an arbiter, eighth stage, or silent second caution engine.
Run selective proof slices at each gate; reserve wider suite execution for late-pack integrity.
Gate 179 whole-repo vocabulary/workbook-path hygiene is mandatory, not optional polish.

LEAF-G171-001.title: Install the integration planning quartet and scope note
ordered_actions: Create the active gates/leaves/execution-log/checklist files for Gates 171-180.
Write the bounded-scope note with the verified master-versus-child framing, the no-blind-patch rule, and the two-tranche merge-then-code order.
Keep Gate 171 planning-only; runtime behaviour remains the Gate 170 closed baseline until a later gate explicitly changes code.
forbidden_actions: Do not start importing child files or editing src/ code in Gate 171.
Do not compress the merge, implementation, proof, and hygiene work into vague placeholder gates.
Do not lose the child handover-note claims or workbook-governance facts from the scope note.
expected_evidence: Active planning quartet and scope note exist with Gates 171-180 and non-placeholder leaves.
Scope note preserves the master/child framing, no-src-delta child finding, and the merge-before-code law.
Bootstrap receipt records the new pack intent, branch state, and exact source artefacts.
definition_of_done: Active quartet exists and the scope note is explicit about tranche order, scope boundaries, and evidence sources.
Bootstrap receipt is written and referenced from the execution log.
LEAF-G171-001.packaging_requirement: record in the active execution log and include a fresh full-history repo zip whenever the operator requests a handoff or the pack reaches a gate closeout

LEAF-G171-002.title: Route the repo truthfully to Gate 172
ordered_actions: Update PLANS.md so the new integration pack is the active pack and the Gate 170 pack is retained as latest closed evidence.
Update the canonical gate map to point paired files and the current active gate marker at Gate 172 in the new pack.
Leave prior closed packs intact as evidence; do not rewrite their internal receipts.
forbidden_actions: Do not leave PLANS.md saying no active pack exists.
Do not erase the closed Gate 170 pack from the router surfaces.
Do not claim the child pack is already merged into master.
expected_evidence: PLANS.md, gate map, and the new leaves ledger all agree that Gate 172 is active on the new branch.
Historical closeout tests for Gates 163, 164, and 170 still pass under the new active-pack state.
definition_of_done: Router/control surfaces are synchronized to the new pack and historical continuity tests accept the successor routing.
LEAF-G171-002.packaging_requirement: record in the active execution log and include a fresh full-history repo zip whenever the operator requests a handoff or the pack reaches a gate closeout

LEAF-G171-003.title: Freeze the integration gate sequence with enough granularity
ordered_actions: Translate the approved integration-and-implementation plan into gate-level sequencing for merge law, planning/reference-data reconciliation, runtime implementation, proofs, hygiene, and final audit.
Keep the child-planning merge tranche separate from the runtime-code tranche so later proof claims stay honest.
Make Gate 179 a mandatory whole-repo vocabulary/workbook-path hygiene gate rather than an optional note.
forbidden_actions: Do not create one giant “merge everything” gate.
Do not omit a final repo-wide hygiene gate.
Do not bury calibration/receipt integration or selective proof order inside prose without gate ownership.
expected_evidence: Gates 172-180 are all materially different and map to the approved plan.
Future leaves contain real ordered actions, validation commands, and definitions of done.
definition_of_done: The new pack can drive later execution one gate at a time without re-planning the whole tranche.
LEAF-G171-003.packaging_requirement: record in the active execution log and include a fresh full-history repo zip whenever the operator requests a handoff or the pack reaches a gate closeout

LEAF-G171-004.title: Write the bootstrap receipt and seed the execution log
ordered_actions: Write a Gate 171 receipt that records the verified master-versus-child facts, the new branch name, and the next active gate.
Seed the execution log with the bootstrap actions and proof slice.
Append a changelog entry for activation of the new integration pack.
forbidden_actions: Do not claim any merge or runtime implementation happened in Gate 171.
Do not omit the child zip and handover note from the bootstrap evidence list.
Do not leave the execution log or changelog stale.
expected_evidence: Bootstrap receipt exists and cites the master and child artefacts plus the no-src-delta finding.
Execution log starts with Gate 171 entries and the proof command/result.
Changelog contains a new gate171 planning entry.
definition_of_done: Receipt, execution log, and changelog all describe the same bootstrap state truthfully.
LEAF-G171-004.packaging_requirement: record in the active execution log and include a fresh full-history repo zip whenever the operator requests a handoff or the pack reaches a gate closeout

Write the manual merge law that decides where master wins, where child wins, and which files must be rewritten by hand.
